title: All code should be reachable
description: All code should be reachableAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEcweunusedWhy is this an issue?More InfoUnreachable code is the code whose statements cannot be executed under any circumstances. Jump statements, likereturn,break,continue, andthrow, alter the normal flow of control within a program, making it possible to skip certain parts of the code, terminate loops prematurely, or exit from functions. So any statements that come after a jump are effectively unreachable.Unreachable statements can be a sign of a logical error or oversight in the program’s design, leading to unexpected behavior at runtime.function func(a) { let i = 10; return i + a; i++; // Noncompliant: this is never executed }Identify and remove unrea
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Code after jump statements (return, throw, break, continue) is unreachable and should be removed.
